text: The video shows evidence of a local perforation and a phlegmon in the appendix region, with visible signs of inflammation and tissue changes.

The surgeon mobilizes the right colon to elevate it, carefully opening the opposite side while maintaining a superficial approach to avoid damaging the cecum.

The surgeon carefully dissects superficially around the appendix, ensuring not to damage the nearby cecum while navigating through the surrounding cecal fat.

The surgeon maneuvers around the anticipated location of the appendix tip, carefully navigating the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon successfully peels the appendix away from the cecum, elevating it into the air. This maneuver provides a clearer view of the mesoappendix, indicating a safe path for further dissection.

The surgeon lifts the appendix away from the cecum, elevating it into the air, which improves visualization of the mesoappendix. This positioning confirms it is safe to proceed with further dissection.

The surgeon attempts to visualize the mesoappendix by repositioning to an inferior view. Using surgical instruments, they transect the anti-mesenteric fat of the terminal ileum to access the base of the appendix and its junction with the cecum.

The surgeon accesses the true isthmus of the appendix, with the tip now free. The base is being cleared of surrounding tissue to prepare for stapling.

The surgeon clears the base of the appendix, ensuring a clean area for stapling after freeing the tip from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon removes the appendix through the umbilical port and irrigates the surgical area. The dissection site is checked for any inadvertent colotomies in the colon, confirming the absence of any damage. The surgical field appears clean after irrigation.
The surgeon removes the appendix through the umbilical port.

The surgeon covers the surgical site with omentum, completing the appendectomy procedure.
